summary: The commenter emphasizes that electronic health records must remain accurate, complete, and trustworthy to ensure patient safety and effective medical decision-making. They caution against deregulation without establishing stronger, modernized audit trail standards that reflect current technology and AI use.
key_arguments: Inaccurate or incomplete records increase preventable medical errors and harm patients.
Audit trails must accurately document data creation and modification, including AI involvement.
Deregulation without stronger standards will worsen existing problems and create new risks.
Electronic health records serve multiple critical functions beyond clinical care, requiring trustworthy data.
recommendations: Modernize and consistently apply foundational standards before any deregulation.
Ensure audit trail standards keep pace with AI use in healthcare.
Maintain strong, enforceable standards to protect patients and support clinicians.
notable_quote: If information can be altered or added later without clear transparency, that record is no longer reliable.
